items.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice

children.content: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.

gallery.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt
A mouse on a stove; a mouse up a wall

thumbnail.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt
A mouse on a stove; a mouse up a wall

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

items.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct

children.content: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

image.altText: A man smiling in a suit.

items.title: Brad Scott confirms Essendon weighed up bumper Harley Reid offer

children.content: Former Essendon coach Brad Scott reveals Essendon weighed up tabling a 10-year, $22 million contract offer to West Coast star Harley Reid, and says he wanted the club to trade Zach Merrett last summer.

image.altText: Brad Scott pictured during his Essendon coaching tenure

items.title: Defence industry could rival WA resources sector under weapons hub plan

children.content: Missiles for use in the Australian military could soon be made in Western Australia with the government seeking to establish a local weapons manufacturing hub.

gallery.altText: A mid-shot of WA Premier Roger Cook giving a speech at an orange dais on stage wearing a suit and tie, against a black backdrop.
Roger Cook and Paul Papalia in discussion as they walk among a crowd at a defence conference, with a blonde woman on the right.

thumbnail.altText: A mid-shot of WA Premier Roger Cook giving a speech at an orange dais on stage wearing a suit and tie, against a black backdrop.
Roger Cook and Paul Papalia in discussion as they walk among a crowd at a defence conference, with a blonde woman on the right.

items.title: Odds of June rate hike close to zero after inflation fall

children.content: Today's inflation announcement reveals rates have eased to below what was expected, suggesting the Reserve Bank may not raise cash rates next month. Follow live.

image.altText: Business people walk past a shiny black sign that reads "Reserve Bank of Australia".

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: 'Inhumane' disability home had fridges locked as residents slept on floor

children.content: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

gallery.altText: a house and a leafy front yard
a photo of a lock on fridge

thumbnail.altText: a house and a leafy front yard
a photo of a lock on fridge

items.title: Why drones fell from the sky during Sydney's Vivid light show

children.content: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 devices fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.

image.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Next steps to take after receiving life-altering ancestry DNA test results

children.content: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

image.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

items.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird shadow

children.content: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

image.altText: James Hird scratches his chin with his right hand

items.title: Farmers hopeful cold front will help WA's dry May problem

children.content: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

gallery.altText: A tractor pulling crop planting equipment in a paddock sits underneath a vibrant and stunning sunset
A photo of farmer Scott Crosby
A photo of Scott Crosby working on his farm at Nyabing

thumbnail.altText: A tractor pulling crop planting equipment in a paddock sits underneath a vibrant and stunning sunset
A photo of farmer Scott Crosby
A photo of Scott Crosby working on his farm at Nyabing

items.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open

children.content: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

image.altText: Adam Walton, wearing a white shirt, holds his hands in the air and smiles.

items.title: 'None of this was forecast': Energy demand from AI data centres soars

children.content: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.

image.altText: a man walks between rows of servers

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, Perth mum Jess says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy to treat PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use at clinics like one in Perth.

gallery.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.
A white pill bottle labelled MDMA
Gloved hands hold a silver tray with pink capsules on it

thumbnail.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.
A white pill bottle labelled MDMA
Gloved hands hold a silver tray with pink capsules on it

items.title: Western Force lick wounds after being ruled out of finals contention

children.content: The Force host the Waratahs in Perth on Saturday night, with the result to decide which team finishes higher than the other in the Super Rugby Pacific standings.

image.altText: Jeremy Williams and Nick Champion de Crespigny shake hands during a Western Force match.

items.title: Should bore water be sold as spring water?

children.content: A resident of the Perth Hills, where thousands of litres of groundwater are extracted each week and sold as bottled water, has questioned whether it's accurate to label it as 'spring water'.

image.altText: Bottled water stacked on fridge shelves.

items.title: Notorious Perth paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon released from jail again

children.content: A notorious Perth paedophile who breached his prison release conditions 11 times has again been freed into the community, as the child protection minister argues it may be unconstitutional to keep him in custody.

items.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Australia's first female astronaut closer than ever to maiden space mission

children.content: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.

gallery.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.
An astronaut with high school students in Kalgoorlie.

thumbnail.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.
An astronaut with high school students in Kalgoorlie.
